_comment: GENERATED from data/model.json by data/generate.py - do not edit by hand. Nodes are get-off points (a coefficient record); each edge flips ONE crucial consideration, recorded in the child's `sets`. Every node ranks the SAME donation slate; only the coefficients change, so 'top_pick' changes as you ride down. 'parent' is the node whose coeffs this one inherits and merges its `sets` onto. 'figures' names the public people who most prominently articulate that stop's worldview. The tree forks at s3_inverts (soil branch A / longtermist branch F), forks again at s4_future (discount / nuclear / astronomical), and ends past s5_astro in the two override stops (Boltzmann brain B, moral anti-realism R).

nodes.desc: Least-crazy end of the line. What matters is humans in my family and local community, in the present generation. Moral weight only on people near me; heavy pure-time discount; humans only; only near-certain, measurable effects count. Distant strangers, animals and the future are outside the circle (weight 0), so the local option wins. The agreed basis for the soup kitchen is a worked BOTEC: people made happier per dollar times their wellbeing gain, netted against the counterfactual of the money sitting in a bank (which still does ~30% as much good). Every node downstream imports THIS number unchanged. The pre-EA commonsense baseline; no single EA figure defends it.

Flip: drop the nationality discount (Peter Singer's famine argument is the origin). What matters is people in the present generation, wherever they live. A life saved abroad counts like a life saved at home, so cheap, measurable global-health and cash interventions dominate. Alexander Berger is the prominent exemplar of deliberately getting off the train here and staying with robust, evidence-backed global health & wellbeing (GiveWell / Open Phil). The soup kitchen's agreed DALY is still carried through untouched; it is simply no longer the best buy.

Flip: extend the moral circle to farmed vertebrates, weighted by a sentience-adjusted welfare range (neuron-count exponent). Peter Singer (Animal Liberation) is the canonical articulator; Lewis Bollard leads Open Phil's farm-animal-welfare work. Corporate welfare campaigns avert enormous suffering per dollar and overtake human global health under most welfare-range estimates.

Flip: count invertebrates, with welfare range ~ (neuron count)^exponent. Brian Tomasik is the prominent voice on insect / invertebrate suffering. This is the worked example of the train's instability: sweeping the exponent from 0 to 2 swings shrimp welfare range across ~12 orders of magnitude and flips the ranking (edit neuron_exponent in this stop's `sets` to watch the top pick move). It is the FORK point: the tree splits here into a soil-animal branch (A) and a longtermist branch (F).

Branch A (biological micro-minds): once you count the ~10^19 soil animals affected by land use, GiveWell top charities' cropland effect works out to ~1.11 kQALY/$ (~1.74x the Shrimp Welfare Project Humane Slaughter Initiative). The 'normal' human charity leaps to ~1.11e5x GiveWell's own direct value - and its SIGN is uncertain (it could be net-negative for soil nematodes). This branch has no Squiggle model yet: there is no comparable published soil-animal BOTEC to copy, so it is not fabricated.

Branch F (longtermist): stop discounting future lives (future_discount stays 1). Toby Ord (The Precipice) and Will MacAskill (What We Owe The Future) are the prominent longtermists; Ajeya Cotra coined the 'train to crazy town' metaphor and rides roughly to here via worldview diversification. Reducing global-catastrophic risk (e.g. ALLFED's civilisational-resilience work) starts to dominate present-focused giving. This node is itself a FORK: below it sit near-identical children that differ by exactly one added term - a pure-time discount, a raised P(nuclear war), or the fanatical x-risk tail. The fanatical tail is still refused here (accept_tiny_prob: false).

Sibling of the longtermist stop, one term different: keep future people in the circle but apply a positive pure-time discount rate (future_discount 0.2). This is the cleanest illustration of the tree's mimicry - the whole calculation is identical to its parent except for a single multiplicative discount term on the future_human domain. A modest discount is enough to pull the winner back toward present, measurable global health.

Another one-term sibling: accept a non-negligible annual probability of nuclear war. The only change from the parent is a catastrophe multiplier (catastrophe_mult 50) that raises the value of future-protecting and resilience work; every other line of the calculation is copied. Civilisational-resilience work (ALLFED) dominates because it directly buys down the tail it now takes seriously.

Accept Nick Bostrom's astronomical-waste argument and take small-probability / huge-payoff bets at face value (accept_tiny_prob flips true, releasing the fanatical tail); Eliezer Yudkowsky pushes AI-risk reasoning to its stark conclusions. Existential-risk reduction dominates everything. This is the far end Cotra's near-termist deliberately gets off before; ride further and the last two stops stop being about charity at all.

nodes.file: Only my own present pleasure is real
nodes.desc: Off the end of the line: if you are more likely a fleeting Boltzmann brain than an embodied agent, the only thing that matters is whatever makes you personally happy right now. This override INVALIDATES every assumption above it: the moral circle, welfare ranges, discounting and x-risk all stop mattering, and every charity collapses to the same low, pleasant-thought level of DALY (boltzmann_level). Nothing you do for anyone else registers, so the ranking goes flat - there is no best buy.

nodes.file: There is no moral fact; better to be selfish
nodes.top_pick: Local soup kitchen  (all negative - keep your money)
nodes.desc: The other terminus: if moral realism is false there is no stance-independent reason to help anyone, so from the only perspective left - your own - every dollar given away is a dollar of personal value lost. This override sets ALL DALYs negative: the whole ranking inverts and the 'best' charity is merely the least self-costly. The consistent recommendation is to keep the money; the soup kitchen shows up on top only because it moves the least.
